menu.title: Learn to Play VTES
menu.subtitle: Play a guided first game of Vampire: The Eternal Struggle in your browser. No rules knowledge needed.

narrator.hint_drag: Click the card, then click its destination

ch1.s0: Quick note before we start: this tutorial works best on a desktop or tablet, where the whole table fits on screen. On a small phone it still runs, but the play regions get cramped. Also, it simplifies a few rules (intercept, some combat details and edge cases); the rulebook is the final word.
ch1.s1: Welcome! In Vampire: The Eternal Struggle (VTES) you are a Methuselah, an ancient vampire. You do not fight directly: you control younger vampires and send them to do your work.
ch1.s2: This is your POOL: 30 blood counters. Pool is both your life and your money. You spend it to bring vampires into play, and if you run out you lose.
ch1.s3: This is your rival's pool. In a full game 4 or 5 players sit in a circle. The player on your left is your PREY and the one on your right is your PREDATOR. In this two player demo your rival is both.
ch1.s4: You score 1 Victory Point by OUSTING your prey, which means bringing their pool to 0. The player with the most victory points wins.
ch1.s5: Tonight you face Sir Walter Nash, Prince of Chicago. Let's learn by playing.

ch2.s1: There are two kinds of cards. First, CRYPT cards: your vampires. This is Aline Gädeke. Click each highlighted part to learn what it means.
ch2.s2: Her NAME, at the top of the card.
ch2.s3: Her CAPACITY: 7. The blood she enters play with, and the most she can hold. Bigger vampires are stronger but cost more.
ch2.s4: Her DISCIPLINES: her powers. They decide which cards she can play. A square icon is the basic level. A diamond icon is the superior level, which also unlocks the stronger bold effect on cards.
ch2.s5: Her CLAN: Brujah. Every vampire belongs to a clan.
ch2.s5b: The card TEXT: her special abilities and her TITLE, Baron of Mannheim. Titles matter for votes, as you will see later.
ch2.s6: The second kind: LIBRARY cards, the cards you play from your hand. This is Legal Manipulations. Let's read it the same way.
ch2.s7: The card TYPE: this is an ACTION card. A vampire plays it when taking an action. Click the icon.
ch2.s7b: One real example of each of the other library types:
ch2.s8: The COST: 1 blood, paid by the vampire that plays it. Some cards cost pool instead, which comes from your own life.
ch2.s9: The REQUIREMENT: Presence. Only a vampire with this discipline can play the card. Aline has it.
ch2.s10: That covers most of what you need to read VTES cards. Let's continue.

ch3.s1: Every player has the same setup. Let's tour your side of the table. Click each highlighted zone.
ch3.s2: Your CRYPT: your vampire cards, face down. At setup you drew 4 of them into your uncontrolled region.
ch3.s3: Your LIBRARY: your deck. During the game you draw from it to refill your hand.
ch3.s4: Your HAND: 7 cards that only you can see. Whenever you play or discard a card, you immediately draw a replacement, so you always hold 7. The only exceptions are cards that tell you not to replace them yet, or running out of library.
ch3.s5: Your UNCONTROLLED region: the 4 face-down vampires you drew at setup, waiting to be paid for. Aline and Leumeah are among them.
ch3.s6: Your READY region: vampires in play stand here and take actions. Empty for now.
ch3.s7: Your ASH HEAP: the discard pile.
ch3.s8: And your POOL. Keep an eye on it at all times. Tour finished, time to play.

ch4.s1: Your turn has 5 phases, always in this order: Unlock, Master, Minion, Influence, Discard. The tracker next to the board shows where you are.
ch4.s2: UNLOCK PHASE: straighten your locked cards so they can act again. Nothing is locked on turn one.
ch4.s3: MASTER PHASE: you may play one master card per turn. Blood Doll needs a vampire in play and you have none yet. Skip.
ch4.s4: MINION PHASE: your vampires act here. You have no vampires in play yet, so nothing happens.
ch4.s4b: One thing before spending influence: you may look at your own uncontrolled cards at any time. Here they are: Aline, Leumeah, and a spare copy of each (real crypts run several copies per vampire). You choose who to pay for. We start with Aline.
ch4.s5: INFLUENCE PHASE: each transfer moves 1 pool onto one of your uncontrolled vampires. To balance going first, transfers ramp up: the game's first turn gets 1, the second turn 2, the third turn 3, and every turn after that 4. This is turn 1, so you have exactly 1 transfer. Click Aline.
ch4.s6: 1 blood on Aline. Discard phase: nothing to do. Turn over.
ch4.s7: Game turn 2 belongs to your rival: he gets 2 transfers and places 2 blood on Sir Walter Nash.
ch4.s8: Game turn 3 is yours, so this time you get 3 transfers. Click Aline to spend them.
ch4.s11: Aline has 4 blood, still short of her capacity of 7.
ch4.s12: Game turn 4: your rival now gets the standard 4 transfers. Nash reaches 6 blood.
ch4.s13: Your turn. From now on everyone gets 4 transfers. Aline needs 3 more blood. Click her.
ch4.s16: 7 blood: her full capacity, she is completely paid. You still have 1 transfer: using it is optional, but unused transfers are lost. Aline is out and you do not want another vampire yet, so let it go.
ch4.s17: Aline comes into play! Careful: she cannot act yet. This turn's minion phase already happened, so she waits, unlocked, until your next turn.
ch4.s17b: DISCARD PHASE: you may discard one card and draw a fresh one. You are holding two copies of Undead Strength, one is plenty. Click a duplicate to discard it.
ch4.s17c: You discard it and immediately draw a replacement, so your hand stays at 7. This is the replace mechanic: play or discard a card, draw back up to 7.
ch4.s18: Your rival only needs 1 more transfer: Nash reaches 7 blood and comes into play too.
ch4.s19: Both players now have a vampire in play. Time for actions.

ch5.s1: Your turn. Unlock phase: nothing is locked, Aline has not acted yet.
ch5.s2: MASTER PHASE: play Blood Doll on Aline. Click Blood Doll in your hand, then click Aline.
ch5.s3: Blood Doll is attached, and it could even be used right now, in this same master phase. Aline is at full blood though, so save it. Notice you drew a new card: whenever you play a card, you draw back up to 7.
ch5.s4: MINION PHASE: Aline can take one action. Basic actions like BLEED or HUNT need no card at all: any ready vampire can do them. Click Aline.
ch5.s5_hunt: She is already at full blood, so hunting would waste her action. Bleeding is how you hurt your prey. Let's bleed.
ch5.s6: Aline LOCKS and announces a bleed. Locking is the price of acting: a locked vampire cannot block or defend until it unlocks.
ch5.s7: Nash could try to BLOCK the bleed, but he chooses to let it through.
ch5.s8: The bleed hits for 1: your prey loses 1 pool. You also seize THE EDGE, the marker held by whoever landed the last successful bleed. It will matter later.
ch5.s9: INFLUENCE PHASE: you have 4 transfers. Let's start paying for your second vampire, Leumeah. Click her.
ch5.s10: One more transfer for now. Click her again.
ch5.s11: That's enough. Keep the rest of your pool: you will need 2 for a gun next turn. Discard phase: nothing to do.
ch5.s12: Your turn ends. Now your rival moves.

ch6.s1: Nash acts: he LOCKS and announces a bleed against YOU.
ch6.s2_take: The bleed resolves and you lose 1 pool. That is allowed, but if you never block, the bleeds add up.
ch6.s2_rewind: Let's rewind and stop him this time.
ch6.s3: Blocking needs a READY and UNLOCKED vampire, but Aline is still locked from her bleed. On the Qui Vive WAKES a locked vampire. Click it in your hand.
ch6.s3b: Aline WAKES. Waking is not unlocking: she stays locked, but she may react and try to block during this one action only.
ch6.s3c: Nash responds with BONDING: at superior Dominate it adds 1 STEALTH (and 1 extra bleed). With more stealth than your intercept, he would slip past the block.
ch6.s3d: Match it with INTERCEPT: play ROOFTOP SHADOW, a Celerity reaction worth 1 intercept. It costs Aline 1 blood. Click it.
ch6.s4: Aline pays 1 blood. 1 intercept cancels 1 stealth: the block SUCCEEDS and the bleed is stopped. You draw replacements for both cards.
ch6.s5: Blocking has a consequence: blocker and attacker now fight. Combat!

ch7.s1: A combat has several steps: set the RANGE, choose STRIKES, resolve them, then the PRESS step decides if another round is fought. In this first fight only the strikes matter, at close range.
ch7.s2: Choose Aline's strike. Her basic hand strike deals 1 damage, but she has SUPERIOR Potence, the diamond icon. Play UNDEAD STRENGTH using its bold superior text: 2 extra damage instead of 1. Click it.
ch7.s3: Strikes happen at the same time: Aline hits for 3, Nash hits back for 1. You draw a new card.
ch7.s4: Damage removes BLOOD, not pool: Nash drops to 4, Aline to 5. A vampire goes to TORPOR if it takes aggravated damage, or if it takes more damage than it has blood to pay. Ending at exactly 0 blood is fine: the vampire stays ready, just hungry.
ch7.s5: Neither side wants another round, so combat ends. Both vampires stay locked.
ch7.s6: Result: you cancelled the bleed and wounded his vampire. Blocking pays off when you can afford it.

ch8.s1: Your turn. UNLOCK PHASE: all your cards unlock and Aline can act again. Nash stays locked until his own unlock phase.
ch8.s2: Master phase: use Blood Doll. Click Aline to move 1 blood from her to your pool.
ch8.s3: You gain 1 pool. Small, steady income like this wins long games.
ch8.s3b: You may also play your one master card of the turn. ANARCH TROUBLEMAKER is a master that stays in play instead of attaching to a vampire. Click it in your hand.
ch8.s3c: It stands in your ready region, in play but attached to nobody. Masters like this wait on the table until the right moment. Keep it in mind.
ch8.s4: MINION PHASE: give Aline a weapon. Equipping is an action. Click the .44 Magnum, then click Aline. The gun costs 2 pool.
ch8.s5: Nash is locked and wounded, so there is no block. Aline locks, pays 2 pool and takes the gun. You draw a new card.
ch8.s6: Equipment stays in play. In her next combat, the Magnum strikes for 2 damage, even at long range.
ch8.s7: INFLUENCE PHASE: 4 transfers to finish paying for Leumeah. Click her.
ch8.s11: 6 blood, her full capacity. Leumeah comes into play! She arrived during the influence phase, so she cannot act until next turn.
ch8.s12: Rival's turn: Nash unlocks and bleeds you for 1. Aline is locked and Leumeah is brand new, so you let this one through, and he takes THE EDGE from you.

ch9.s1: Your turn: TWO ready vampires and a master in play. Unlock phase: everything of yours unlocks.
ch9.s2: Master phase: Blood Doll again. Click Aline to move 1 blood to your pool.
ch9.s4: MINION PHASE. Aline opens with a simple bleed. Click her.
ch9.s5: The bleed hits: your rival loses 1 pool and you take THE EDGE back from him.
ch9.s6: Now Leumeah, your other Baron, calls the vote. Play EAT THE RICH, a political action. Click it.
ch9.s7: Leumeah locks and announces the political action.
ch9.s8: Before any voting, blocking gets its chance: a political action can be blocked like any other action, and political actions carry +1 STEALTH by default, which makes them harder to catch. Nash is still locked, so nothing stops the referendum.
ch9.s9: The action succeeds, so now the terms are chosen. Pick the target of Eat the Rich:
ch9.s9_opt_rival: Your rival, who controls a rich Prince
ch9.s9_self: Targeting yourself would only hurt you. The card punishes players who control TITLED vampires, like Nash, Prince of Chicago. Target your rival.
ch9.s10: Votes are counted one by one. The political action card itself gives 1 vote to the caller's controller: you. Score: 1 in favor, 0 against.
ch9.s11: Nash votes against. A Prince is worth 2 votes, but Eat the Rich takes 1 vote from every titled non-priscus vampire (a priscus loses a ballot instead). Score: 1 to 1. A tie fails.
ch9.s12: Your rival discards ANARCHIST UPRISING, a political action from his own deck, to buy 1 extra vote against. Score: 1 to 2.
ch9.s13: Your Barons answer. Each is worth 2 votes, minus 1 from the card. Aline can vote even while locked: locked vampires still vote. Click her.
ch9.s14: Aline and Leumeah add 1 vote each. Score: 3 to 2. That would already pass, but you can push further.
ch9.s15: You hold THE EDGE. Burning it normally gives 1 vote, but Eat the Rich's text makes it worth 2 here. Click the Edge to burn it.
ch9.s16: Score: 5 to 2. The referendum PASSES by 3. Note that you cannot also discard a political card for a vote: each player gets at most 1 vote from political cards, and yours came from the card Leumeah called.
ch9.s17: Your rival loses 4 pool: 1 as the base effect, plus 3 for controlling a titled vampire.
ch9.s18: The reward: Leumeah called the vote and won it, so she can play VOTER CAPTIVATION. Click it.
ch9.s19: It passed by 3 votes, so Voter Captivation gains 3 blood. At superior Presence, up to 2 of them go to your POOL. The third would refill Leumeah, but she is already full. Win by more, gain more.
ch9.s20: One more trick. Aline's own card text lets her burn 1 blood to UNLOCK after another Anarch you control completes a successful action. Leumeah just did exactly that. Click Aline.
ch9.s21: Aline burns 1 blood and unlocks. She could act again this turn or block during your rival's turn. The abilities printed on your vampires are always there.
ch9.s22: Politics paid off: pool damage, blood back, and a wounded rival. But Nash is still standing. Next: a combat in full detail to remove him.

ch10.s1: Same game, a few turns later. Everyone rebuilt: Blood Doll and hunting refilled your side, and Nash is back to 6 blood. Time to remove him for good, and to fight a combat in full detail. Watch the tracker above the board: every ROUND has a RANGE, STRIKE and PRESS step.
ch10.s1b: MASTER PHASE first. Aline only has basic Celerity, so she cannot use the strong version of some cards. Play the CELERITY master card on her: it adds a level of Celerity (and 1 capacity). Drag it onto Aline.
ch10.s1c: Aline now has SUPERIOR Celerity. That unlocks the bold text of Celerity cards, which matters in a moment. You draw a replacement card too.
ch10.s2: MINION PHASE. Aline bleeds Nash. Click her.
ch10.s3: Nash BLOCKS this time. Both vampires lock, and combat begins.
ch10.s4: Round 1, RANGE step: combat starts at close range, but each combatant may play MANEUVERS to change it.
ch10.s5: The .44 Magnum gives Aline one optional maneuver per combat. Where do you want to fight?
ch10.s5_close: At close range Nash hits back with his hand strike. With a gun, distance is your friend. Go long.
ch10.s6: LONG RANGE. Guns shoot at any range; hand strikes and fangs only work up close.
ch10.s7: STRIKE step: click Aline to shoot.
ch10.s8: 2 damage: Nash burns 2 blood. His hand strike cannot reach you at long range.
ch10.s9: Nash has had enough: he strikes with MAJESTY instead. Combat ends, no damage is done, and no ordinary press can continue it.
ch10.s10: One card can: PSYCHE! at superior Celerity queues a NEW combat with the same opponent when combat is about to end. Click it.
ch10.s11: A brand new combat begins against Nash. Everything resets, even the Magnum's once-per-combat maneuver.
ch10.s12: RANGE step: use the Magnum's maneuver to go to long range again.
ch10.s14: 2 more damage: Nash is down to 2 blood and still cannot reach you.
ch10.s15: PRESS step: this time press normally. Play your second PSYCHE!, its basic level is a simple press. Click it.
ch10.s16: Round 2 of the new combat, and the range resets to close. Time for the finisher.
ch10.s17: Play BURNING WRATH: a hand strike with 2 extra AGGRAVATED damage, 3 in total. It costs 3 blood. Click it.
ch10.s18: Nash strikes back with his hand for 1 damage and plays RESILIENCE to prevent damage: its superior level prevents 3 NON-aggravated damage, useless against Burning Wrath, so he uses the basic level and prevents just 1.
ch10.s19: 2 aggravated damage get through: the first WOUNDS Nash (aggravated cannot be mended) and he burns 1 blood to survive the second. Had the aggravated damage exceeded his blood, he would be BURNED outright, straight to the ash heap.
ch10.s20: A wounded vampire goes to TORPOR at the end of the round. Nash collapses with 1 blood left.
ch10.s21: A complete combat: range, strikes, presses, Majesty, superior Psyche!, aggravated damage and prevention. Nash is out of the fight, but the night is not over.

ch11.s1: Your rival fought back. While Nash lay in torpor, TIMOTHY CROWLEY, another Prince, came into play and RESCUED him (any ready vampire can rescue, even the rival's). Then Crowley caught Leumeah alone: a brutal fight sent her to TORPOR with 0 blood.
ch11.s2: Your turn. In torpor Leumeah cannot act or defend, and enemies could even DIABLERIZE her there, draining her completely. Let's get her out.
ch11.s3: Any ready vampire can RESCUE a vampire in torpor as an action. Click Aline.
ch11.s4: Aline locks for the rescue attempt. Crowley could block it, he stays put.
ch11.s5: The rescue costs 2 blood, paid by the rescuer, the rescued, or split between them. Who pays?
ch11.s5_split: You could split it, but Leumeah has 0 blood, she cannot pay anything. Aline pays.
ch11.s6: Aline pays 2 blood and Leumeah returns to the ready region: hungry, but free. Being rescued does not lock or unlock her, and she is no longer wounded.
ch11.s7: Now a mandatory rule kicks in: a ready, unlocked vampire with NO blood MUST hunt. You cannot take any other optional action, with any vampire, until Leumeah has hunted. Click her to HUNT.
ch11.s8: Leumeah locks and hunts: 1 blood back. The mandatory hunt is done, the rest of your minions are free to act again.
ch11.s9: Torpor handled: rescue, pay, recover. Now let's get you some help.

ch12.s1: The rival kept pressing: Crowley bled you and Nash hunted. Your turn, everything unlocks. In your hand waits a PROCURER, an ALLY: a minion that is not a vampire.
ch12.s2: Aline recruits the PROCURER (Leumeah is too low on blood to pay). Recruiting is an action, and this ally's cost is paid in BLOOD by the recruiting vampire: 2 blood. Click the card in your hand.
ch12.s3: Aline locks and pays 2 BLOOD, and the Procurer enters play with 1 LIFE, 1 strength and 0 bleed. Allies lose LIFE instead of blood, and at 0 life they are burned.
ch12.s4: From now on the ally acts like any minion: it can bleed, block, and use its printed abilities. This one can carry fresh blood to your vampires. But not yet: minions cannot act on the turn they come into play.
ch12.s5: A quick rival turn: Crowley LOCKS to bleed you for 1. Your freshly recruited Procurer just stands there: minions cannot act on the turn they come into play.
ch12.s6: Your turn again. UNLOCK PHASE: all your cards straighten up, so Aline, locked from recruiting, is ready once more.
ch12.s6b: And now the Procurer can act: it carries 1 blood to Aline. Small helpers add up.
ch12.s7: You know allies now. One night remains: the kill.

ch13.s1: Several nights pass. You keep bleeding, voting and feeding; your rival claws for survival but keeps sinking. He is down to 3 pool. Tonight it ends.
ch13.s2: UNLOCK PHASE, and one last trick waiting on the table: the ANARCH TROUBLEMAKER.
ch13.s3: It is used during your unlock phase. Click it.
ch13.s4: As the card commands, you hand it to your rival (it moves to his side unlocked, it is not a card that locks) and lock up to two of HIS vampires. Crowley AND Nash lock: his whole defense is gone.
ch13.s5: MINION PHASE. Nobody can block. Finish it.
ch13.s6: Play LEGAL MANIPULATIONS, a bleed action with 2 extra bleed. Click it.
ch13.s7: Aline pays 1 blood, locks and bleeds for 3.
ch13.s8: Your prey reaches 0 pool: OUSTED! You gain 1 Victory Point and a reward of 6 pool.
ch13.s9: That is Vampire: The Eternal Struggle, from the first transfer to the oust: pool, actions, blocks, combat, politics, torpor and allies. In a real five player game the hunt continues around the table. You are ready. Go play!

resources.title: Your next steps
resources.subtitle: You know the basics. Here is everything you need to go further.
resources.decks_cta_text: Browse preconstructed decks: ready-made starting points you can buy or proxy.
resources.rulebook_text: The complete Fifth Edition rules, free from Black Chantry.
resources.what_to_buy_text: The community's guide to your first purchases.
resources.codex_text: The strategy school: archetypes, articles and deck guides.
resources.vekn_text: Find events, local playgroups and the official forum.
resources.discord_text: The most active place to ask questions and find online games.
resources.succubus_text: Play VTES online against other players.
